reasoning: The spark has a genuinely charming pixel art palette — cyan/blue outer scatter, warm yellow/orange mid-ring, white hot center — which reads as energetic and game-friendly. On dark backgrounds (panels 2 and 3) contrast is excellent and the starburst silhouette is instantly readable. However, on the light/cream background (panel 4), the white-yellow center bleeds into the bg, losing the hot-core punch. At 1x (panel 1), the shape compresses to a faint star blob — recognizable as a spark but personality is lost. Silhouette is spiky/explosive by nature, which is appropriate for a hit VFX but diverges from the 'round/soft friendly toy' ideal. Not charming-but-mushy (the pixel edges are crisp), not charmless (the color palette has real warmth and playfulness), but the light-bg contrast issue and 1x micro-legibility keep this at marginal rather than a pass.
